message:
🧩 Use of PLINEEDIT:

Edits polylines efficiently.

💡 Tip: Convert lines into polylines easily.

Steps:
1. Type PEDIT
2. Select line
3. Convert to polyline

📐 Use of BOUNDARY:

Creates closed polylines automatically.

💡 Tip: Perfect before hatching.

Steps:
1. Type BOUNDARY
2. Pick inside area

📊 Use of REGION:

Converts closed shapes into regions.

💡 Tip: Needed for area calculations.

Steps:
1. Type REGION
2. Select objects

⬛ Use of SOLID:

Creates filled shapes.

💡 Tip: Faster than hatch for small fills.

Steps:
1. Type SOLID
2. Pick corners

🧽 Use of WIPEOUT:

Hides background objects.

💡 Tip: Great for clean text areas.

Steps:
1. Type WIPEOUT
2. Draw boundary

📏 Use of TEXTALIGN:

Aligns text neatly.

💡 Tip: Keeps notes clean.

Steps:
1. Type TEXTALIGN
2. Select text

🔄 Use of LAYTRANS:

Maps layers between drawings.

💡 Tip: Useful when merging files.

Steps:
1. Type LAYTRANS

📉 Use of FLATTEN:

Converts 3D objects to 2D.

💡 Tip: Fix imported drawings.

Steps:
1. Type FLATTEN
2. Select objects

🛠️ Use of AUDIT:

Fixes drawing errors.

💡 Tip: Run weekly for clean files.

Steps:
1. Type AUDIT
2. Confirm repair

🚑 Use of RECOVER:

Opens damaged drawings.

💡 Tip: Saves corrupted files.

Steps:
1. Type RECOVER
2. Select file

📐 Use of ISOPLANE:

Switches isometric views.

💡 Tip: Essential for isometric drawings.

Steps:
1. Press F5

✂️ Use of SECTIONPLANE:

Creates cross-sections in 3D.

💡 Tip: Inspect interiors.

Steps:
1. Type SECTIONPLANE

📦 Use of PRESSPULL:

Extrudes enclosed areas.

💡 Tip: Fast 3D modeling.

Steps:
1. Type PRESSPULL
2. Click area

⬆️ Use of EXTRUDE:

Turns 2D into 3D solids.

💡 Tip: Used in modeling.

Steps:
1. Type EXTRUDE

🔄 Use of REVOLVE:

Creates 3D objects by rotation.

💡 Tip: Great for pipes.

Steps:
1. Type REVOLVE

🌀 Use of SWEEP:

Moves profile along a path.

💡 Tip: Create rails & tubes.

Steps:
1. Type SWEEP

📐 Use of LOFT:

Connects shapes into solids.

💡 Tip: Advanced modeling.

Steps:
1. Type LOFT

➖ Use of SUBTRACT:

Cuts solids from others.

💡 Tip: Boolean modeling.

Steps:
1. Type SUBTRACT

➕ Use of UNION:

Joins 3D solids.

💡 Tip: Merge parts.

Steps:
1. Type UNION

🔀 Use of INTERSECT:

Keeps common solid area.

💡 Tip: Advanced boolean work.

Steps:
1. Type INTERSECT

🎨 Use of MATERIALS:

Applies textures in 3D.

💡 Tip: Realistic views.

Steps:
1. Type MATERIALS

🌄 Use of RENDER:

Creates realistic images.

💡 Tip: Present designs.

Steps:
1. Type RENDER

☀️ Use of SUNSTATUS:

Controls lighting.

💡 Tip: Better shadow study.

Steps:
1. Type SUNSTATUS

🌍 Use of GEOGRAPHICLOCATION:

Sets real world location.

💡 Tip: Accurate sunlight.

Steps:
1. Type GEOLOCATION

📏 Use of ANNOTATIVESCALE:

Controls scale automatically.

💡 Tip: Avoid resizing text.

Steps:
1. Type ANNOAUTOSCALE

🚶 Use of LAYWALK:

Preview layers quickly.

💡 Tip: Understand drawings faster.

Steps:
1. Type LAYWALK

⏰ Use of TIME:

Shows drawing time info.

💡 Tip: Track productivity.

Steps:
1. Type TIME

⚙️ Use of OPTIONS:

Customizes AutoCAD settings.

💡 Tip: Improve workflow.

Steps:
1. Type OPTIONS

🛠️ Use of CUSTOMIZE:

Edits UI and shortcuts.

💡 Tip: Faster commands.

Steps:
1. Type CUI

🧰 Use of TOOLPALETTES:

Quick tool access.

💡 Tip: Save time.

Steps:
1. Press Ctrl+3

⚡ Use of QUICKSELECT:

Selects similar objects.

💡 Tip: Bulk edits.

Steps:
1. Type QSELECT

🎯 Use of SELECTSIMILAR:

Selects same type objects.

💡 Tip: Instant selection.

Steps:
1. Type SELECTSIMILAR

🔍 Use of FIND:

Finds text in drawings.

💡 Tip: Edit notes quickly.

Steps:
1. Type FIND

✏️ Use of RENAME:

Renames layers, blocks.

💡 Tip: Organize projects.

Steps:
1. Type RENAME

〰️ Use of SPLINE:

Creates smooth curves.

💡 Tip: For organic shapes.

Steps:
1. Type SPLINE

🌀 Use of HELIX:

Creates spiral shapes.

💡 Tip: Springs & ramps.

Steps:
1. Type HELIX

⭕ Use of TORUS:

Creates donut shapes.

💡 Tip: 3D rings.

Steps:
1. Type TORUS

⚪ Use of SPHERE:

Creates spheres.

💡 Tip: Balls & domes.

Steps:
1. Type SPHERE

🛢️ Use of CYLINDER:

Creates cylindrical solids.

💡 Tip: Pipes & tanks.

Steps:
1. Type CYLINDER

🔺 Use of CONE:

Creates cone solids.

💡 Tip: Funnels & roofs.

Steps:
1. Type CONE

📦 Use of BOX:

Creates box solids.

💡 Tip: Quick 3D blocks.

Steps:
1. Type BOX

🔺 Use of PYRAMID:

Creates pyramid solids.

💡 Tip: Roof structures.

Steps:
1. Type PYRAMID

🔄 Use of CONVTOSOLID:

Converts surfaces to solids.

💡 Tip: Fix imported 3D.

Steps:
1. Type CONVTOSOLID

🧵 Use of SURFNETWORK:

Creates surfaces between curves.

💡 Tip: Organic models.

Steps:
1. Type SURFNETWORK

🌊 Use of SURFBLEND:

Blends surfaces smoothly.

💡 Tip: Smooth transitions.

Steps:
1. Type SURFBLEND

📦 Use of THICKEN:

Adds thickness to surfaces.

💡 Tip: Convert to solid.

Steps:
1. Type THICKEN

🔪 Use of SLICE:

Cuts 3D objects.

💡 Tip: Section models.

Steps:
1. Type SLICE

🥚 Use of SHELL:

Hollows 3D objects.

💡 Tip: Lightweight models.

Steps:
1. Type SHELL

📏 Use of OFFSETEDGE:

Offsets 3D edges.

💡 Tip: Refine solids.

Steps:
1. Type OFFSETEDGE

⭕ Use of FILLETEDGE:

Rounds 3D edges.

💡 Tip: Smooth corners.

Steps:
1. Type FILLETEDGE

📐 Use of CHAMFEREDGE:

Bevels 3D edges.

💡 Tip: Sharp detailing.

Steps:
1. Type CHAMFEREDGE

📌 Use of PROJECTGEOMETRY:

Projects curves onto surfaces.

💡 Tip: Advanced modeling.

Steps:
1. Type PROJECTGEOMETRY

📐 Use of ALIGNSPACE:

Aligns model to layout.

💡 Tip: Viewport fix.

Steps:
1. Type ALIGNSPACE

🖼️ Use of MVIEW:

Creates viewports.

💡 Tip: Paper space control.

Steps:
1. Type MVIEW

🔄 Use of LAYOUTREGEN:

Refreshes layouts.

💡 Tip: Fix display bugs.

Steps:
1. Type LAYOUTREGEN

📤 Use of PUBLISH:

Batch prints drawings.

💡 Tip: Multi-sheet plots.

Steps:
1. Type PUBLISH

📝 Use of EATTEDIT:

Edits block attributes.

💡 Tip: Faster than ATTEDIT.

Steps:
1. Type EATTEDIT

🔄 Use of FIELDUPDATE:

Refreshes fields.

💡 Tip: Update values.

Steps:
1. Type FIELDUPDATE

📐 Use of DWGUNITS:

Sets drawing units quickly.

💡 Tip: Fix scaling errors.

Steps:
1. Type DWGUNITS

📄 Use of VIEWBASE:

Creates drawing views from 3D.

💡 Tip: Engineering drawings.

Steps:
1. Type VIEWBASE
